rationale: In the citing paper, Ailawadi et al. (2018) is not just listed as background; it is explicitly used in the methods for exploratory basket-composition analyses. The authors state they classify receipt items using category values and procedures from that paper, then report results based on those constructs. This is a clear case of methodological and operational application in a new context (handheld scanner use), even if it is not the main theoretical base of the article.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Ailawadi et al. (2018) as a measurement source, using its category-level ratings and coding approach to characterize purchased items (eg, healthfulness, impulsiveness, hedonicity, stockpilability) in Study 2. These borrowed measures support the paper's analysis of how scanner use changes basket composition.
